item.title: 'The Com' Cyberattacks Support Violence & Sexploitation
item.description:
Your organization's security failures have consequences for everyone else as well since this criminal gang uses its cyber winnings to support more violent and widespread crimes.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: With Complex Cloud Integrations, Small Errors Lead to Major Compromises
item.description:
Researchers discover an exploit chain combining over-permissioned roles, secrets discovery, and non-human identities that could have compromised a popular automation service.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Asia's Cyber Insurance Market Shows Signs of Life
item.description:
The cyber insurance industry has made relatively weak inroads into Asia due to a variety of factors, but that could be changing.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: As Global Powers Explore Humanoid Robots, Cyber-Risk Looms
item.description:
The future of cybersecurity is germinating, as nation-states vie for dominance in the embodied AI market and its supply chain.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Name That Toon: Mark of (Cybersecurity) Progress
item.description:
As part of Dark Reading's 20th anniversary package, we asked readers for a cybersecurity-related caption that captures their thoughts about the industry's last two decades.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Patch Now: Another Palo Alto Auth Bypass Bug Under Active Exploit
item.description:
Exploiting the PAN-OS GlobalProtect VPN vulnerability requires certain conditions, but adversaries have done so in two attack waves that started in mid-May.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Microsoft's Zero-Day Legal Threats Spark Backlash
item.description:
After a disgruntled security researcher published several zero-day exploits in recent weeks, Microsoft seemingly indicated criminal charges were in order.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Recent Palo Alto Networks Vulnerability Exploited for Weeks
item.description:
Hackers began exploiting CVE-2026-0257, an authentication bypass in Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS, four days after public disclosure. The post Recent Palo Alto Networks Vulnerability Exploited for Weeks appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: 19-Year-Old Linux Kernel Vulnerability Exposes Systems to Root Access
item.description:
Proof-of-concept (PoC) exploit code has been released for the CIFSwitch flaw, which allows low-privileged users to escalate to root on vulnerable Linux systems. The post 19-Year-Old Linux Kernel Vulnerability Exposes Systems to Root Access appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Dragos Acquires xIoT Security Firm Phosphorus
item.description:
Dragos said customers will soon gain expanded asset visibility and integrated device intelligence, with automated remediation workflows and a unified platform experience to follow. The post Dragos Acquires xIoT Security Firm Phosphorus appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Critical Windows Netlogon Vulnerability in Attackers’ Crosshairs
item.description:
Organizations are advised to patch CVE-2026-41089 as soon as possible, given its severity, the potential ongoing exploitation. The post Critical Windows Netlogon Vulnerability in Attackers’ Crosshairs appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: WP Maps Pro Vulnerability Exploited to Take Over WordPress Sites
item.description:
The security defect (CVE-2026-8732) allows unauthenticated attackers to create administrative accounts on the affected installations. The post WP Maps Pro Vulnerability Exploited to Take Over WordPress Sites appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Dashlane Brute-Force Attack Leads to Limited Encrypted Vault Downloads
item.description:
Dashlane’s security systems automatically locked accounts to protect them against the hacking attempts. The post Dashlane Brute-Force Attack Leads to Limited Encrypted Vault Downloads appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Supply Chain Attack Hits 32 Red Hat NPM Packages
item.description:
Hackers published 96 malicious package versions, injected with a credential-stealing worm similar to Mini Shai-Hulud. The post Supply Chain Attack Hits 32 Red Hat NPM Packages appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Unknown hacker group targeted Russian maritime universities, diplomats for nearly two years
item.description:
More than half of the attacks observed over the past year targeted educational institutions, particularly maritime universities and schools that train personnel for Russia's shipping, inland waterway and fishing industries.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Afghan finance officials targeted by suspected Pakistani cyberespionage campaign
item.description:
A suspected Pakistan-linked hacking group has targeted Afghanistan's Ministry of Finance and provincial government officials in a new cyberespionage campaign, researchers have found.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Microsoft says it will not pursue security researchers after zero-day backlash
item.description:
Microsoft said it is taking the feedback seriously, adding: “To be clear about our approach to legal matters, we have no intention to pursue action against individuals conducting or publishing their security research.”
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: NSA selects new leads for key cybersecurity posts
item.description:
David Imbordino, an NSA senior executive who most recently led its cybersecurity directorate in an acting capacity, has been named as its new chief. Bruce Jones, a career NSA technical and operational leader, as the new head of its Cybersecurity Collaboration Center.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Inspector general finds NIST mistakes have made vulnerability database ineffective
item.description:
NIST’s National Vulnerability Database (NVD) backlog mushroomed from 13,000 unprocessed security vulnerabilities in February 2024 to more than 27,000 by the end of 2025, “undermining the NVD’s utility and public trust," according to an inspector general report.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
item.description:
A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
item.description:
An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
item.description:
Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
item.description:
Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
item.description:
Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequ
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
item.description:
The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: OCR Reports to Congress on HIPAA Compliance and Data Breaches in 2024
item.description:
The Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) Office for Civil Rights (OCR) has submitted its annual reports to Congress […] The post OCR Reports to Congress on HIPAA Compliance and Data Breaches in 2024 appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: The Oncology Institute Confirms Vendor Breach Involved Patient Data
item.description:
The Oncology Institute, a publicly traded provider of cancer care through more than 100 clinics in California, Oregon, Nevada, Arizona, […] The post The Oncology Institute Confirms Vendor Breach Involved Patient Data appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Connecticut Medicaid Portal Breach Affects 22,500 Hartford HealthCare Patients
item.description:
The personal and protected health information of approximately 22,500 Hartford HealthCare patients has been exposed in a security incident. Data […] The post Connecticut Medicaid Portal Breach Affects 22,500 Hartford HealthCare Patients appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Lakeview Health Systems Settles Class Action Data Breach Lawsuit
item.description:
A settlement has been negotiated to resolve a class action lawsuit against Lakeview Health Systems LLC. The lawsuit stemmed from […] The post Lakeview Health Systems Settles Class Action Data Breach Lawsuit appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Healthcare Orgs Lack Confidence in Ability to Defend Against an AI-incited Identity Breach
item.description:
Healthcare organizations have embraced AI and are using AI agents to perform a range of functions, including handling IT support […] The post Healthcare Orgs Lack Confidence in Ability to Defend Against an AI-incited Identity Breach appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: HIPAA Security Rule Training Requirements
item.description:
The HIPAA Security Rule training requirements mandate HIPAA-Covered Entities and HIPAA Business Associates to provide workforce security awareness training that […] The post HIPAA Security Rule Training Requirements appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: California AG Files Lawsuit Over 23andMe Data Breach
item.description:
California Attorney General Rob Bonta has filed a lawsuit against the genetic testing company formerly known as 23andMe over its […] The post California AG Files Lawsuit Over 23andMe Data Breach appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Medical Billing Company Data Breach Affects 7 Medical Groups
item.description:
The Las Vegas medical billing and coding management company, La Perouse, has announced a data breach that has affected seven […] The post Medical Billing Company Data Breach Affects 7 Medical Groups appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: UK Visa Portal spilled thousands of applicants’ passports and selfies online — and hasn’t fixed the leak
item.description:
Zack Whittaker reports: A website called UK Visa Portal is publicly exposing the passports and selfie photos of applicants who signed up and paid the site to obtain a U.K immigration visa, TechCrunch has learned. An anonymous person notified TechCrunch about the security lapse, saying that the website is exposing at least 100,000 documents from... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Silent Ransom Group Impersonating IT Personnel through Social Engineering
item.description:
The FBI has issued a Flash Alert about the Silent Ransom Group. Summary The Silent Ransom Group (SRG), also known as Luna Moth, Chatty Spider, and UNC3753, is targeting law firms using social engineering techniques. Through phone calls and phishing emails, SRG actors pose as IT support to establish access to victim computers and exfiltrate... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: NL: Schiphol cargo worker arrested over alleged data leaks to drug networks
item.description:
NL Times reports: The Royal Netherlands Marechaussee detained a 24-year-old Amsterdam-based cargo worker at Schiphol on Tuesday, May 19, on suspicion of unauthorized access to computer systems and the leaking of confidential company information, Luchtvaart Nieuws has reported. According to the ongoing investigation, the suspect allegedly used his access to a cargo handling company’s systems at the... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Romanian National Sentenced for Selling Access to Networks of Oregon State Government Office
item.description:
A Department of Justice press release on May 27 reports that a Romanian national who faced seven years in prison for selling access to an Oregon state government office in 2021 and other U.S. entities has been sentenced to 56 months in prison: According to court documents, Catalin Dragomir, 46, formerly of Constanta, Romania, sold... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: California AG Bonta Sues Chrome Holding Co., Formerly Known as 23andMe, Over 2023 Data Breach
item.description:
Jaimie Ding reports: Attorney General Rob Bonta filed the lawsuit against Chrome Holding Co., which 23andMe rebranded under after filing for bankruptcy last March. 23andme is known for its direct-to-consumer DNA test kits that provided customers information on their ancestry and genetic predispositions for certain health conditions. The lawsuit calls for various civil penalties against 23andMe... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: French Health Payments Breach Exposed ID Data, Fuels Fraud Fears
item.description:
Michel Gribouille reports: A major French health-care payments middleman says hackers broke into a key authorization portal and may have exposed sensitive personal data, including France’s equivalent of a Social Security number, setting off warnings about identity theft and scam attempts. Almerys, a company that helps process “third-party payment” transactions so patients don’t have to... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Thousands of Oregon prison files accessed by prison worker
item.description:
Noelle Crombie reports on today’s reminder of the insider threat: A former Snake River Correctional Institution employee accessed tens of thousands of Oregon Department of Corrections files over a six-month period last year, the agency announced Friday. Officials discovered the data breach in January during an investigation into misconduct allegations involving the unnamed employee, according to a statement... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Microsoft’s incident response is getting a failing grade from researchers (1)
item.description:
Microsoft is ticking off a lot of researchers this week by claiming that those who dump proof-of-concept exploits for vulnerabilities they have not responsibly disclosed are enabling criminal activity, and that Microsoft will track them and bring cases against them. Whoever advised them to issue that statement may want to walk it back. (See update... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Bombay High Court Issues Injunction Prohibiting Hackers From Publishing Allegedly Hacked HDFC Investor Data (1)
item.description:
The Bombay High Court granted interim relief to HDFC AMC after a ransomware group called “Morpheus” allegedly stole over 680 GB of sensitive company and investor data. The court barred unidentified hackers from publishing or sharing the information, warning that any leak could lead to identity theft, financial fraud and irreparable harm. The case will... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Alberto Daniel Hill’s Cybermidnight Coverage of the Latin American Digital Sovereignty Crisis (March–June 2026)
item.description:
Alberto Daniel Hill’s report is a must-read for anyone who wants to begin to understand what is going on in Argentina, Uruguay, and Mexico with respect to digital security. One of the many limitations of being a solo blogger is that there are entire areas of the world or sectors I basically know nothing about... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Palo Alto GlobalProtect VPN auth bypass flaw now exploited in attacks
item.description:
Palo Alto Networks is warning that hackers are now exploiting a PAN-OS GlobalProtect authentication bypass flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-0257, in attacks attempting to breach corporate networks. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Critical Windows Netlogon RCE flaw now exploited in attacks
item.description:
The Centre for Cybersecurity Belgium (CCB), the country's national authority for cybersecurity, warned on Friday that threat actors are now exploiting a recently patched critical Windows Netlogon vulnerability in attacks. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Dashlane password manager users locked out by brute force attacks
item.description:
Multiple Dashlane users have been locked out of their accounts following brute-force attacks that attempted logins from distant locations and unknown devices. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Spain arrests doxer leaking sensitive data of govt employees
item.description:
The Spanish National Police has arrested an individual for leaking sensitive information related to members of various key state organizations, including the National Cybersecurity Institute (INCIBE). [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Red Hat npm packages compromised to steal developer credentials
item.description:
More than 30 npm packages under Red Hat's '@redhat-cloud-services' namespace were compromised in a supply-chain attack that distributed a new variant of the Shai-Hulud credential-stealing malware, dubbed "Miasma." [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Hackers hijack thousands of sites for ClickFix and FakeUpdate attacks
item.description:
A threat actor tracked as DriveSurge has been operating large-scale malware distribution campaigns using ClickFix and FakeUpdates techniques on compromised sites. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Computime Group Report Victim computime.com Threat Actor TheGentlemen Date Discovered Jun 01, 2026 Description Computime Group Ltd. is an electronics engineering and manufacturing â¦June 01, 2026
item.description:
Computime Group Report Victim computime.com Threat Actor TheGentlemen Date Discovered Jun 01, 2026 Description Computime Group Ltd. is an electronics engineering and manufacturing â¦June 01, 2026 reported a ransomware. Attributed to TheGentlemen.
🎭 Threat Actor: TheGentlemen | 📰 Source: BreachSense | 🏷️ Type: Ransomware

item.title: Unknown
item.description:
Unknown reported a healthcare breach.
📰 Source: HHS OCR | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Industrial Acceptance Corporation
